decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to control the game through counterspells and removal spells while utilizing faeries and ninjas to maintain card advantage and tempo. The deck aims to keep the opponent on the back foot by disrupting their game plan and eventually winning through efficient creature beatdown with backup burn spells. Snow lands and spells also enable powerful removal and burn options. The deck combines aggro-control elements to disrupt and control the opponent's strategy, while maintaining card advantage and board presence.

This deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent through a combination of direct burn spells and aggressive creatures. By efficiently utilizing low-cost spells and creature abilities, it aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. The aggressive nature of the deck is further reinforced by cards that provide additional value when casting spells, allowing for explosive turns and maintaining pressure on the opponent. With a fast and efficient game plan, this deck aims to close out the game quickly before the opponent can mount a defense.

The deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient Elves and generating card advantage with cards like Lead the Stampede and Winding Way. It utilizes mana acceleration from elves like Priest of Titania and Llanowar Elves to play powerful threats like Nyxborn Hydra and Timberwatch Elf. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Masked Vandal and Avenging Hunter to deal with opponent's threats. Wellwisher provides additional resilience against aggressive strategies, while Generous Ent can provide a significant boost in power.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of aggressive Elf creatures, leveraging mana ramp and card draw to maintain a steady flow of threats. The key strategy is to quickly build up a wide board presence, buffing creatures with Timberwatch Elf and attacking relentlessly to pressure the opponent. The deck also includes utility creatures like Masked Vandal and wellwisher to deal with opponent's threats and gain life to prolong the game. Overall, the deck aims to consistently apply pressure and outvalue the opponent with efficient creature synergy.

The main theme of this deck is to control the game through card advantage and tempo manipulation. The deck utilizes cards like Mulldrifter and Sunscape Familiar to draw cards and reduce the cost of spells, while also including counterspells like Prohibit and Negate to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of Ephemerate allows for value plays and protection of key creatures like Archaeomancer. The deck aims to outlast the opponent while generating incremental advantages over time, ultimately grinding out a win through efficient card usage and solid board control.

This deck focuses on using a combination of Gates lands and control spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect its own creatures. By using cards like Prismatic Strands and Counterspell, it aims to control the board and prevent the opponent from gaining momentum. The inclusion of creatures like Guardian of the Guildpact and Outlaw Medic offer additional protection and threats on the battlefield. The deck also features card draw and creature tutoring to maintain consistency and adaptability in different game situations. Overall, the strategy revolves around being reactive and strategic to outmaneuver the opponent and secure victory.

The deck aims to quickly fill the graveyard with creatures using cards like Satyr Wayfinder and Stinkweed Imp, then return them to the battlefield with Dread Return. Scrapwork Mutt and Lotleth Giant can deal massive damage to the opponent once they are reanimated. The combo of Malevolent Rumble and Generous Ent can provide additional value by sacrificing creatures. The deck also includes graveyard recursion options like Mortuary Mire to ensure a steady stream of reanimation targets. Overall, the deck relies on a strong synergy between graveyard manipulation and powerful reanimation effects to overwhelm the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly swarm the battlefield with small, low-cost creatures and overwhelm the opponent with numbers. By using cards like Battle Screech, Mardu Devotee, and Kor Skyfisher, the deck can generate a wide board presence early in the game. Cards like Thraben Charm and Raffine's Informant provide additional utility and flexibility in combat situations. The goal is to maintain pressure on the opponent and close out the game quickly with aggressive attacks and powerful combat tricks.

The main focus of this Mono Blue Aggro deck is to quickly put pressure on the opponent with efficient creatures like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent, while using counterspells like Force Spike and Counterspell to protect your own threats. The deck also includes card draw spells like Brainstorm and Ponder to maintain card advantage and keep up the pressure. Sleep of the Dead can be used to tempo out the opponent and close out the game quickly. This deck aims to be fast and disruptive, overwhelming the opponent before they can stabilize.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly to unleash powerful creature spells and control the game. By assembling the Urzatron lands, the deck can generate a significant amount of mana to cast its high-cost creatures like Maelstrom Colossus and Bramble Wurm. Additionally, the deck includes control elements like Relic of Progenitus and Pinnacle Kill-Ship to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with large threats while maintaining control of the game through efficient mana ramp and disruptive spells.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of aggressive Elves. By utilizing cards such as Avenging Hunter, Timberwatch Elf, and Nyxborn Hydra, the deck aims to quickly build a strong board presence and outpace the opponent. With mana acceleration from Llanowar Elves and Priest of Titania, the deck is able to ramp up quickly and efficiently. Additionally, cards like Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help maintain card advantage and keep the Elf army flowing. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around aggressive, efficient creature plays to overrun the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly summoning low-cost white creatures to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of weenies. By utilizing cards like Kor Skyfisher and Militia Bugler to continuously generate creatures and Battle Screech for additional tokens, the deck aims to maintain a high level of pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Prismatic Strands and Guardians' Pledge helps to protect the creatures and boost their power, creating a formidable aggro strategy that aims to win the game through sheer numbers and speed.

The main concept of this Aggro - Affinity deck is to quickly swarm the board with artifact creatures, while also utilizing cards that benefit from having a high artifact count on the battlefield. The deck aims to pressure the opponent with aggressive plays and efficient damage output, while also having some card draw and removal options to maintain tempo and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It relies on synergies between artifacts and cards that support the Affinity mechanic to overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost, aggressive creatures and disruptive spells in order to control the game. By utilizing cheap spells like Brainstorm, Counterspell, and Force Spike, along with efficient creatures like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of card draw spells like Thought Scour and Ponder helps maintain a steady flow of threats and answers, while Sleep of the Dead provides a powerful finisher to secure victory.

The key concept of this deck is to utilize the combo between Balustrade Spy and Dread Return to quickly mill the entire library, allowing the player to return Lotleth Giant from the graveyard and deal massive damage to the opponent. The deck focuses on ramping up mana with cards like Gatecreeper Vine and Overgrown Battlement, while also using cards like Winding Way and Lead the Stampede to draw into combo pieces. Generous Ent can provide additional card advantage, while Masked Vandal deals with any problematic artifacts or enchantments. Additionally, Saruli Caretaker helps to fix mana and Lotus Petal can provide a burst of mana in a pinch.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly play multiple low-cost creatures and control spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan and deal damage early on. By utilizing efficient counter spells and bounce effects, the deck aims to apply pressure and maintain tempo advantage. The inclusion of card draw spells like "Of One Mind" and "Ninja of the Deep Hours" allows the deck to keep up its momentum and maintain a steady stream of threats. Overall, the deck is built to be fast, aggressive, and disruptive in order to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize.
